title: pinus sylvestris subsp. sibirica
intro: Pinus sylvestris subsp. sibirica, also known as Siberian pine, is a subspecies of the European black pine tree native to Siberia and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Siberian pine include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
